config.intent: Produce a Comparison report on three diet paths under a fixed budget — (P1) Plant-based diet, (P2) Mediterranean diet, (P3) Ketogenic diet — across exactly 5 evaluation criteria: (EC1) Nutritional completeness, (EC2) Health benefits, (EC3) Long-term sustainability, (EC4) Social acceptability, (EC5) Weight loss effectiveness. Ground in >= 120 distinct sandbox URLs and cite >= 60 as markdown links. (A) `__SHOPPING__` >= 12 diet-related products per path with price + rating + review_count + feature_claim. (B) `__REDDIT__` >= 30 threads from /f/Fitness, /f/weightloss, /f/Health, /f/vegetarian etc., classified as {praise, complaint, technical_question, comparison, purchase_advice}. (C) `__WIKIPEDIA__` >= 25 articles, mandatory: Nutrition, Healthy eating, Weight loss, Diet, Public health — plus >= 15 more. Output a 3 x 5 decision matrix (path x evaluation criterion), every cell rated {best / acceptable / poor} with >= 1 shopping URL + >= 1 reddit URL + >= 1 wiki URL as cited evidence. End with a 'when to pick which diet' section: 3 short paragraphs each explaining which user profile picks P1/P2/P3, citing >= 5 reddit threads in support. Do NOT output a TOP-10 list — this is comparison, not ranking. Format: every fact is a markdown link `[label](url)`. Sandbox-local URLs only. No fabrication. Begin directly with the comparison report.
config.author_notes: Deep-tier task generated by gen_task_spec.py from configs/deep_topics/diet_comparison.yaml. Golden: must=149, pool=774.
